headline: Put your standards in the path of AI work.
subhead: Every review starts from your published standards. Not improvisation.

hero_facts.label: What it is
hero_facts.value: A governance layer that applies your team's workflows, guardrails, and examples to AI-generated drafts before the next pass.

hero_facts.label: What you get
hero_facts.value: A structured review packet with keep, fix now, escalate, and the v2 brief, so the next generation starts from evidence instead of guesswork.

hero_facts.label: How teams use it
hero_facts.value: Install JudgmentKit in your AI client, run the refinement loop against a draft, and keep your team's review standards in the path from first pass to final decision.

workflow_heading: From first draft to next pass.

workflow_steps.title: Add it to your client
workflow_steps.body: Install JudgmentKit in the AI client your team already uses so agents can call the same review layer over MCP.
workflow_steps.review_focus: Check that the draft captures the current section order, hero, CTA path, proof content, and raw artifact exposure.

workflow_steps.title: Review the draft
workflow_steps.body: Run the review against a real draft so JudgmentKit returns what to keep, what to fix now, and what to escalate.
workflow_steps.review_focus: Confirm the refinement run is still anchored to workflow.ai-ui-generation.

workflow_steps.title: Generate the next pass
workflow_steps.body: Use the returned brief to get a stronger next draft instead of another round of prompt thrash.
workflow_steps.review_focus: Make sure the refinement run cites the right guardrails and the onboarding-drift example before rewriting.

workflow_steps.title: Rewrite toward v2
workflow_steps.body: The second pass should answer what JudgmentKit is and how to start before it spends screen space on inspect surfaces, proof chains, or raw artifacts.
workflow_steps.review_focus: The v2 brief should bias toward clarity and first-time usability rather than novelty.

workflow_steps.title: Surface review and escalation
workflow_steps.body: Anything that still wants new primitives, unusual structure, or unclear tradeoffs becomes an explicit review item instead of being improvised into the page.
workflow_steps.review_focus: A reviewer should be able to see what was fixed, what remains open, and why.

dogfood_support: This page dogfoods the same refinement loop a team would run after choosing JudgmentKit: a compact first-pass draft, a copy-distinctness review, the UI copy clarity guardrail, and the repetitive-copy example all feed the next v2 brief.
proof_support: Each review stays anchored to a published workflow, explicit guardrails, and a calibration example.
outcomes_support: JudgmentKit improves the next AI-assisted pass before your team spends another hour prompting, rewriting, or reviewing by hand.

outcomes.title: Clearer next-step decisions
outcomes.description: JudgmentKit shows what to keep, what to fix now, and what to escalate so people make review decisions from the same packet.

outcomes.title: Less prompt thrash
outcomes.description: Workflows, guardrails, and calibration examples load with the run, so teams stop restating the same standards in every prompt.

outcomes.title: More consistent reviews
outcomes.description: Teams review AI drafts against the same standards across clients, operators, and projects instead of relying on whoever happened to check.

context_heading: Your standards load before generation starts
context_support: Workflows, guardrails, and calibration examples load before the review runs, so the process begins with your team's structure instead of the model's defaults.
final_heading: Connect first. Use raw references only when you need proof.
